instruction: Walk up stairs. At top of stairs turn right. Walk straight to bedroom. Turn left and walk to bed lamp.Turn left and enter closet. Stop at rug.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Walk up stairs. At top of stairs turn right. Walk straight to bedroom. Turn left and walk to bed lamp.Turn left and enter closet. Stop at rug. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I see a foyer area. I need to find the stairs. Let me look around to locate them.
I can see stairs. The "left" view (+270°) shows stairs going up clearly. The "behind" view also shows stairs. Let me turn left to face the stairs and walk up.
I'm facing the stairs. The stairs go up to the left. There's a plant/railing in the center. Let me adjust to align better with the stairs going up on the left side.
Now I'm facing the stairs directly and they go up ahead of me. Let me walk up the stairs.
The stairs curve to the right as they go up. Let me continue up, following the curve.
The stairs continue curving up to the right. Let me keep following them up.
The stairs continue up ahead. Let me continue climbing.
I've reached the top of the stairs. Now the instruction says to turn right. Let me look around to understand my surroundings at the top.

I'm at the top of the stairs. The instruction says "At top of stairs turn right." 

Looking at the views:
- Ahead: a room with a dresser and mirror, open floor
- Right (+90°): looks over the stairwell railing 
- Left (+270°): shows a hallway/opening leading to another room

The instruction says turn right and walk straight to bedroom. Let me first step forward a bit to be fully at the top landing, then turn right. Actually, let me turn right and see where a bedroom might be.

Let me turn right and observe.

This view faces the railing overlooking the stairwell — that's not toward a bedroom. The right direction here overlooks the balcony. Let me reconsider. 

Coming up the stairs, I ended facing "ahead" (a room with dresser/mirror). The bedroom is likely that direction or through one of the openings. Let me look back at the ahead view. The room ahead with the dresser could be the bedroom. Let me turn back to the ahead view and consider the openings.

Actually, let me think about orientation. When I finished the stairs, "ahead" showed a room. Turning right from that put me facing the railing. Perhaps the bedroom is through the opening on the left of the ahead view. Let me turn back to explore the openings that lead to rooms.

Now I can see through an opening on the right there's a room that looks like it has a bed (a bedroom). There's a dresser/mirror on the left. Let me move forward toward the top landing and explore. First let me step forward off the stairs to be fully on the landing floor.

Now I can clearly see a bedroom to the right (through the opening) with a bed. The instruction says at the top of stairs turn right and walk straight to bedroom. The bedroom is to my right. Let me turn right toward the bedroom opening and walk in.
I can see the bedroom entrance ahead-left with the bed. Let me turn slightly left to align with the doorway and walk into the bedroom.
The bedroom is straight ahead through the doorway. Let me walk forward into the bedroom.
I'm approaching the bedroom doorway. The bed is ahead-right. Let me continue forward into the bedroom.
There's a wall/doorframe close in center. The bedroom opening is to the right. Let me turn right to enter the bedroom through the doorway.
Now I can see the bedroom clearly ahead — the bed is to the left, and there are windows and a dresser. Let me walk forward into the bedroom.

I'm now inside the bedroom. The bed is on the left with nightstands on either side. The instruction says "Turn left and walk to bed lamp." I can see the bed on the left. Let me turn left to face the bed and find the bed lamp (there appear to be lamps on the nightstands).
I turned too far and I'm now facing a curtain/wall. The bed and lamp are to the right. Let me turn back right a bit to face the bed with its nightstand lamp.

Now I can see the bed clearly with a bed lamp on the nightstand (left side of the bed). The instruction was "Turn left and walk to bed lamp." I'm now oriented toward the bed. 

I can see the bed lamp on the nightstand to the left of the bed. Let me approach it. The lamp is on the near nightstand. Let me move toward the bed/lamp area, then the instruction continues "Turn left and enter closet."

Let me move forward toward the bed lamp.

Now I can clearly see the bed lamp on the nightstand (the white lamp on the left nightstand). I'm now near the bed lamp. The instruction says "Turn left and walk to bed lamp. Turn left and enter closet."

I'm approaching the bed lamp. Now I need to turn left and enter the closet. Let me get a bit closer to the lamp first, then look for the closet. Let me look around to find the closet.

I'm now right at the bed lamp — the white lamp on the nightstand is close to my left. I've reached the bed lamp. Now the instruction says "Turn left and enter closet." Let me look around to find the closet, which should be to my left.

Now I can see the layout:
- Ahead: the bed with lamp
- Right (+90°): windows and dresser with mirror
- Behind (+180°): doors, one appears to be an open closet/room
- Left (+270°): a hallway/passage leading to what could be a closet area or bathroom

The instruction says "Turn left and enter closet." My left view shows a passage/hallway. Let me turn left and explore that direction to find the closet.

I'm now facing left toward a hallway/passage. There's a doorway ahead. This leads to another area. Let me walk forward toward it to find the closet. There's a wall opening on the right (the passage). Let me move forward through the passage.
I'm in a hallway/passage area outside the bedroom. There are several doors. On the left I can see what looks like a closet area with mirrors/shelving. Let me look around to identify the closet.

Now I understand the layout better:
- Ahead: a hallway with an area on the left that looks like a closet (with mirror/shelving)
- Right (+90°): back to the bed
- Behind (+180°): the dresser and window area
- Left (+270°): a wall/doorframe, with a passage visible to the far left

The closet appears to be ahead-left. In the "ahead" view I can see through an opening on the left there's a room with mirrors and shelving — that's likely the closet/walk-in closet. Let me move toward it.

Excellent! Now I can see a rug on the floor ahead, and there's a walk-in closet area on the left (with built-in shelving/cabinetry). The instruction says "enter closet. Stop at rug." 

I can see a white rug on the floor ahead. There's a closet to the left with built-in shelves. Let me move toward the closet and the rug. Let me walk forward.
I can see the closet on the left (with built-in shelving), and a rug is at the bottom of the view. The closet entrance is to the left. Let me turn left to enter the closet.

Now I can see the walk-in closet clearly with built-in shelving/cabinetry. There's a rug visible on the right (the fluffy white rug). The closet extends ahead. Let me enter the closet — there's an opening on the right leading into the closet space. Let me move forward into the closet.
I'm now inside the walk-in closet with shelving on the left. There's a rug visible on the right. Let me turn right slightly to see the rug and the closet interior better.

I'm in the closet. There's a doorway ahead. The rug seems to be in the entrance area behind me. Let me look at where the rug is. Earlier I saw a white fluffy rug near the closet entrance. Let me continue into the closet interior to find the rug. Let me move forward a bit into the closet space.

I'm deeper in the closet now. There's shelving on the left. The floor here is dark wood, no rug directly here. The rug I saw earlier was at the closet entrance. Let me look around to locate the rug precisely.

Now I understand the layout better:
- Ahead: continuing into a closet section
- Right (+90°): more closet shelving with folded items and a door
- Behind (+180°): the entrance area with the bedroom visible, and the shelving
- Left (+270°): more built-in closet shelving

I'm inside the closet with built-in shelving all around — this is clearly the walk-in closet. The rug I saw earlier was near the closet entrance (behind me / at the entrance). Looking at the "behind" view, I can see back toward the entrance. The white rug was at the entrance to this closet area.

I recall the white fluffy rug was right at the closet entrance opening. Let me go back toward the entrance where the rug was. The behind view shows the entrance passage. Let me turn around and head to the rug at the closet entrance.

Now I'm facing back toward the closet entrance/bedroom. I can see the bedroom through the passage on the left. The closet shelving is on the right. The rug should be in the entrance area. Let me move forward toward the entrance where I saw the white rug.

I'm now near the closet entrance, looking back toward the bedroom. The closet shelving is on the right. The rug I saw earlier was located near the closet entrance/vanity area. 

Let me reconsider — the white fluffy rug I saw earlier was in the hallway area between the bedroom and the closet, near where I entered. Let me look toward that entrance area. The rug was near the passage. Let me move forward slightly and check the floor for the rug.

I'm now at the closet entrance/exit near the bedroom. I can see the bed to the left and the closet shelving to the right. I've moved back close to the bedroom. 

I think I overshot — the rug was in the closet entry passage. Let me reconsider the original view. When I was approaching the closet, I saw the white fluffy rug on the floor at the passage. Looking at the current view, the floor ahead is dark wood.

Let me check the floor immediately. Let me turn and look at the entrance area floor to locate the white rug. Let me look behind me and to the left where the rug/vanity area was.

I'm now facing the closet cabinetry/shelving. This is the inner closet. The rug was actually in the entry area near the vanity where I first saw it. Let me recall: when I was at tool call 49, I saw a white rug on the floor ahead as I approached this whole closet/dressing area. 

Let me reconsider the geometry. The rug appeared to be in the middle of the dressing room passage. Let me go back to that entrance area. The rug was where I entered this dressing/closet complex. Let me turn around and look at the entry floor.

Now I see the layout: on the left is a closet section with dark shelving/cabinet, and the bedroom bed is on the right. I'm in the passage between closet and bedroom. 

The rug I saw was at the closet complex entrance. Let me recall the view at tool call 49 — it showed a white rug in the middle of the dressing area passage, near the mirror/hallway. That was where I first entered this dressing area from the bedroom hallway. 

Let me continue turning to find the entrance where the rug was. The entrance with the rug was toward where I came from (back toward the hallway/passage). Let me turn to look for the rug location.

Now I can see the entrance area with a mirror on the left and doors. This is the entry to the dressing/closet room. The rug should be nearby. I see there's an opening ahead-left. Let me move forward toward where I entered, where the rug was located.

I see a door ahead and a mirror on the left. This is the closet/dressing area entrance. I recall the white rug was in the entry passage. Let me look to the left where there's an opening (the passage with the mirror).

Now I can see the white rug! It's on the floor ahead in the entry passage (the light-colored rug visible in the center of the image, on the floor near the doorway/mirror). This is the rug at the closet entrance. Let me move toward it.

I can now see the rug clearly on the floor ahead — the light beige/white rug in the doorway/passage area. It leads to what appears to be a bathroom beyond. I'm very close to the rug now. Let me step forward onto/to the rug.

texts: {"clearance_m": {"left": 0.5, "center": 1.3, "right": 0.8}, "tool_calls_used": 80, "tool_calls_remaining": 20, "BUDGET_WARNING": "Only 20 tool calls remain before this session is killed. Stop exploring new areas; commit to your best goal candidate, approach it, and STOP before the budget runs out."}

text:
I can see the rug directly ahead on the floor at the doorway threshold. I'm right at the rug now. Wait — this leads to a bathroom, not a closet. Let me reconsider.

Looking back: The instruction was "enter closet. Stop at rug." I already entered the closet (the walk-in closet with shelving). The rug at the closet — I saw a white fluffy rug earlier at the closet entrance. 

This current view shows a rug leading to a bathroom. But the closet was the space with built-in shelving I was in. The rug I need is the one at the closet. 

However, I'm now right at a rug on the floor. Given the budget warning, and that I've entered the closet/dressing complex and am at a rug, I'm within the target area. The rug is directly ahead of me. Let me step forward onto the rug and stop.

text:
I've now entered a bathroom (with bathtub, shower, vanity). This is not the closet. I overshot the closet rug. The rug I'm on now / just passed was the threshold rug into the bathroom.

Let me reconsider. The instruction endpoint is "enter closet. Stop at rug." The closet was the walk-in closet with the built-in shelving that I was in earlier. There was a white fluffy rug near that closet entrance. 

I've now passed through into the bathroom. I need to go back to the closet. The closet with shelving was behind me. Let me turn around and go back to the closet area where the rug was.

Given budget constraints, let me back up to the closet. The closet entrance rug was where I was at tool call 49-51. Let me turn around.

text:
Now I can see the walk-in closet area clearly. There's a white rug on the floor ahead of me (the light rug in the lower portion of the image). The closet has built-in shelving on the right, and there's an opening to the closet on the left with more shelving. The white rug is right in front of me in this dressing/closet room.

This is the closet area with the rug. Let me move forward onto the rug and stop.

text:
I'm now in the walk-in closet/dressing area with built-in shelving on the right. I can see the bedroom through the doorway ahead. The rug I saw was on the floor just below/behind me now. Let me check the floor — I may be standing right on the rug now.

I'm in the closet area, which is the target. The rug was right where I moved through. I'm now within the closet at the rug location. This is the endpoint: "enter closet. Stop at rug."

I'm confident I'm in the closet at the rug. Let me issue STOP.
